spec_match.rationale: Correctly demonstrates the current standard 'none', 'all', 'start', and 'end' values side by side.

interactive.rationale: The page now has a width slider, preset buttons, and a live line-fragment readout so users can force different wrap points.

blurb_alignment.rationale: Matches the parent index's description of comparing none, all, start, and end side by side to see how underlines disappear or join.

edge_cases.rationale: The dynamic width control lets users test narrow, issue-width, and wide wrap points while comparing all four skip-space values.

references.rationale: Includes direct references to ChromeStatus, the CSS Text Decoration 4 property definition, and the motivating CSSWG GitHub issue.

design_system.rationale: Aligned perfectly with design styles, standard borders, serif font selections, and stone backgrounds.

summary: The wrapped-inline page lets visitors resize the sample paragraphs, choose wrap presets, and watch live line-fragment counts while comparing none, all, start, and end behavior.
